title: Hybrid Retrieval & Reranking
summary: BM25 + a semantic stand-in, fused with RRF — offline, polyglot, identical rankings in Python, Node and .NET.

elements.text: BM25 and embeddings are not enemies — they solve different retrieval problems. BM25 wins exact terms (error codes, IDs); embeddings win paraphrases. Fuse both with RRF.

elements.note: Interactive hybrid-search web UI — type a query, see all three rankings.
Offline test of the lesson's claims.
The whole demo: tokenize, BM25, semantic stand-in, RRF.
One sample document from the tiny corpus.

elements.text: Test it on a richer document: `./run -l 3 web` opens an interactive search over the 5-chapter fictive story (The Voyage of Caretta the Magnificent). Watch how an exact name vs a paraphrase pick different chapters.

elements.note: One chapter of the fictive-story corpus the web UI searches.
